comprehension_gate.instruction: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION: Before any implementation work, read this entire prompt. Then present a structured summary to the Lead Architect: (a) Milestone objective in your own words (b) List of work items with 1-sentence descriptions (c) Files you will create or modify (d) Validation strategy (e) Any risks or ambiguities you see Then STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval. Do NOT create a branch, write files, or run commands until explicit approval is received.

milestone.title: Task 7 / EA-1: Policy Agent Audit
milestone.predecessor: Task 6: Test Governance Framework (ledger Entry 38, commit 51d3031)
milestone.objective: Audit the policy_agent test suite against docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md and record findings in a new consolidated document at docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md. EA-1 is limited to services/policy_agent/src and services/policy_agent/tests. It must answer the four Task 7 audit questions for policy_agent only: coverage gaps, stale/dead tests, assertion quality, and boundary correctness. It must also append ledger Entry 39 to docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md. No production files or test files may be changed.
milestone.scope: Overall Task 7 audit surface: 33 test files and 37 production files across 8 test domains. This EA-1 slice is limited to: - services/policy_agent/src: 10 production Python files (excluding __init__.py) - services/policy_agent/tests: 12 test files (excluding __init__.py)
milestone.deliverable_rule: EA-1 CREATES docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md. EA-2 through EA-4 APPEND additional service/domain findings. EA-5 NORMALIZES the full document and writes the final Prioritized Gap Report and Pre-existing Skip Analysis.
milestone.tiered_methodology: Tier 1 (MANDATORY): inventory and coverage map Tier 2 (MANDATORY): full per-file scan for stale tests, assertion quality, and boundary correctness Tier 3 (CONDITIONAL FAIL-SAFE): if you cannot complete the assigned scope without quality degradation, STOP and produce a partial report plus recommended re-scope. Do NOT rush remaining files.

file.description: Create the consolidated Task 7 findings document. For EA-1, populate the policy_agent findings only.
Append ledger Entry 39 documenting completion of the policy_agent audit slice.

forbidden_changes.item: Do NOT modify any file under services/policy_agent/src/
Do NOT modify any file under services/policy_agent/tests/
Do NOT modify docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md for this audit slice
Do NOT create or modify conftest.py
Do NOT add, remove, rename, or fix tests
Do NOT run coverage tooling or install dependencies
Do NOT touch the pre-existing unrelated untracked files in the worktree

item.title: Inventory policy_agent production and test files
item.description: Build the authoritative policy_agent file inventory before drawing conclusions.
item.instructions:
Confirm the full scope is exactly these production files: - adjudicator.py - boot.py - car.py - config_loader.py - constants.py - entrypoint.py - gpu_inference.py - ipc.py - jwt_minter.py - rule_engine.py
Confirm the full scope is exactly these test files: - test_adjudicator.py - test_boot.py - test_car.py - test_config_loader.py - test_entrypoint.py - test_gpu_inference.py - test_hybrid_adjudicator.py - test_integration_car_pipeline.py - test_ipc.py - test_jwt_minter.py - test_rate_and_resource_rules.py - test_rule_engine.py
Exclude __init__.py from the coverage count, but note its existence if relevant to import-path or staleness findings.

item.title: Create the Coverage Map for policy_agent
item.description: Map every policy_agent production file to its test file(s) and assign a coverage assessment.
item.instructions:
In docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md create a top-level section exactly named: ## Coverage Map
Under it, create a subsection: ### policy_agent
Add a table with these columns: | Production File | Test File(s) | Coverage Assessment | Rationale |
Use these assessment rules consistently: - COVERED: direct test coverage exists and the module appears to satisfy the governance expectation of primary path plus at least one error/edge path for its public behavior. - PARTIAL: some direct or indirect coverage exists, but important public behavior, error handling, or key branches appear untested or under-tested. - NONE: no meaningful corresponding tests were found; incidental coverage from unrelated tests does not count.
This table must include one row for each of the 10 production files.

item.title: Audit stale or dead policy_agent tests
item.description: Identify tests that reference dead code, stale imports, removed module paths, or superseded behavior.
item.instructions:
In docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md create a top-level section exactly named: ## Stale Test Inventory
Under it, create a subsection: ### policy_agent
Review all 12 policy_agent test files and cross-check every tested symbol, import path, class name, and expected behavior against current production code.
Flag: - references to symbols that no longer exist - imports from dead or renamed modules - tests that still encode superseded architectural assumptions - tests whose stated purpose no longer matches the current implementation
For each finding, record: - test file - stale symbol/path/behavior - current production reality - why it is stale or dead
If no stale/dead findings exist for policy_agent, state that explicitly.

item.title: Audit assertion quality in policy_agent tests
item.description: Identify weak assertions and tests that prove too little.
item.instructions:
In docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md create a top-level section exactly named: ## Assertion Quality Findings
Under it, create a subsection: ### policy_agent
Evaluate all 12 policy_agent test files for weak assertion patterns, including: - assert True - bare truthiness assertions without checking value/behavior - assert result is not None when the actual semantic value matters - try/except patterns that swallow failures - tests that only inspect internal implementation details rather than observable behavior - tests whose assertions are too shallow to detect meaningful regressions
Produce: - a short policy_agent summary paragraph - a flat list of specific findings with test file and rationale
If no material assertion-quality findings exist, state that explicitly.

item.title: Audit boundary correctness for policy_agent tests
item.description: Determine whether policy_agent tests behave like true unit tests under the governance rules.
item.instructions:
In docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md create a top-level section exactly named: ## Boundary Violations
Under it, create a subsection: ### policy_agent
Evaluate whether any policy_agent tests: - make real socket connections - perform real filesystem writes beyond acceptable isolated test scaffolding - depend on live services, hardware, or manual setup - should really be integration tests rather than service-level unit tests
Apply the Task 6 governance rule strictly: service-level tests under services/*/tests/ should be unit tests unless there is a compelling reason they belong elsewhere.
For each violation or borderline case, record: - test file - observed behavior - why it violates or strains the intended boundary - recommended reclassification or remediation direction
If no material boundary violations exist for policy_agent, state that explicitly.

item.title: Create the initial consolidated findings document correctly
item.description: EA-1 must create docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md in a form that later audit sessions can append to cleanly.
item.instructions:
docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md must contain these exact top-level sections in this order:
1. ## Coverage Map 2. ## Stale Test Inventory 3. ## Assertion Quality Findings 4. ## Boundary Violations 5. ## Prioritized Gap Report 6. ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis
For EA-1: - Populate sections 1–4 with policy_agent findings only. - In sections 5 and 6, insert a brief explicit placeholder: "Deferred to EA-5 synthesis." - Add a short introductory note near the top stating that the document is being built across EA-1 through EA-5 and that EA-1 covers policy_agent only.
Do NOT write the final prioritized remediation ordering yet. Do NOT disposition the two pre-existing skipped tests yet.

item.title: Record ledger Entry 39
item.description: Append Entry 39 documenting completion of the policy_agent audit slice.
item.instructions:
Follow the existing ledger format.
Required content: - Title: Entry 39 — Task 7: Test Quality Audit / EA-1 Policy Agent Audit (COMPLETE) - Date: 2026-04-18 or actual execution date - Branch: feature/p5-task7-ea1-policy-agent-audit - Predecessor: Entry 38 — Task 6: Test Governance Framework - Type: AUDIT / DOCS-ONLY - Disposition: COMPLETE - Summary: EA-1 completed; docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md created; policy_agent coverage map, stale-test findings, assertion-quality findings, and boundary-correctness findings recorded; global prioritization and skip analysis deferred to EA-5. - Files: docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md only

item.title: Enforce strict DOC_ONLY branch hygiene
item.description: The worktree already contains unrelated untracked files. Preserve them and stage only the two allowed docs files.
item.instructions:
Do NOT use git add .
Stage only: - docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md - docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md
The authoritative DOC_ONLY gate for this milestone is the staged diff against main. After staging only the two allowed files, the diff must show only those files.

item.title: Tier 3 fail-safe
item.description: If the full policy_agent audit cannot be completed to quality within one session, stop cleanly.
item.instructions: If you determine mid-session that you cannot finish all 10 production files and 12 test files without degraded quality: - STOP - leave a partial but clearly labeled report in docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md - record the partial completion status in ledger Entry 39 - provide a recommended re-scope for the SDO Do NOT compress the remaining review into superficial findings.

context_notes.note: docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md is the governing standard for this audit. Use its scope definitions, marker policy, boundary rules, and coverage expectations as the audit rubric.

pyproject.toml defines: - testpaths including services/policy_agent/tests and tests/integration - addopts = "--strict-markers -v --import-mode=importlib -m 'not slow'" - registered markers: asyncio, slow For policy_agent service tests, no slow marker usage is expected.

Numeric coverage tooling is out of scope. This audit is based on careful code-to-test mapping and qualitative adequacy against the Task 6 governance rule: public behavior should have happy-path and error/edge-path test coverage.
The worktree contains unrelated untracked artifacts. Do not touch, stage, rename, or delete them. They are outside this milestone.
This is a diagnosis-only audit. The EA must not fix any finding, add any test, or modify any source/test file.
The final Prioritized Gap Report and the final Pre-existing Skip Analysis are not written in this slice. EA-1 only creates placeholders for those sections.

gate.criteria: No Python files may be created or modified in this milestone.

Do not run pytest as a standard gate for this slice. Use file inspection as the primary evidence source. If you choose to run a targeted read-only command to confirm a suspected stale reference, report it explicitly.

gate.command: Manual audit completion against the 10 production files and 12 test files in scope
gate.criteria: All 10 policy_agent production files are mapped in the Coverage Map and all 12 policy_agent test files are evaluated for staleness, assertion quality, and boundary correctness.
The document contains all 6 required top-level sections in order. Sections 1–4 contain policy_agent content. Sections 5–6 explicitly say "Deferred to EA-5 synthesis."
If the full scope was not completable without quality degradation, the EA stopped, produced a partial report, and recommended a re-scope instead of rushing.

verification.instruction: The Lead Architect will use terminal commands only. Provide a result set that these commands can confirm.

commands.expected: Single EA-1 audit commit on the feature branch
commands.command: Select-String -Path docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md -Pattern "^## ","^### policy_agent","Deferred to EA-5 synthesis"
commands.expected: Shows all 6 required top-level sections, policy_agent subsections, and the deferred placeholders
Coverage Map contains qualitative assessments for policy_agent production files
Ledger contains Entry 39 for Task 7 / EA-1

commit_template.message:
Task 7 EA-1: Audit policy_agent test quality
- Created docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md - Added policy_agent Coverage Map, Stale Test Inventory, Assertion Quality Findings, and Boundary Violations - Deferred Prioritized Gap Report and Pre-existing Skip Analysis to EA-5 synthesis - Recorded ledger Entry 39 in Post-Operational Maturation Ledger
DOC_ONLY gate: staged diff against main contains only docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md

attachments.instruction: Attach the following files when creating the EA-1 session:

file.reason: Root pytest configuration and marker policy source

attachments.follow_on_note: Starting with EA-2, docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md becomes a required attachment as well.
